steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows the study's key finding: democratic strength restrained inflation only when paired with inequality. The conclusion must explain what this finding means for the Norway-Cameroon comparison that opened the passage — specifically, whether that comparison supports or undermines the idea that democratic institutions are bad at controlling inflation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — the factors that contributed to Norway's elevated inflation rate relative to Cameroon's have less to do with the countries' political institutions than with the countries' levels of income inequality.: too strong — the study shows inequality interacts with democratic strength; it doesn't eliminate institutions as a factor. ✗
  • Choice B — speculations about the relative inability of democratic institutions to counteract inflation are based on measures that tend to exaggerate the levels of inflation in strongly democratic countries such as Norway.: introduces a methodology critique the passage never raises. ✗
  • Choice C — it would be a mistake to treat the relative inflation rates of Norway and Cameroon as indicative of an inherent shortcoming in democratic institutions with regard to control over inflation.: matches exactly — the study shows democracy can restrain inflation when inequality is present, so the two-country comparison doesn't prove democracy is an obstacle to inflation control. ✓
  • Choice D — the difference between Norway and Cameroon with regard to democratic institution strength may have been greater in 2016 than was represented by international indices.: disputes index accuracy, which the passage never questions. ✗
That is the correct answer.
